hero.titleLead: If I have seen further, it is by standing on the
hero.subtitle: Physics isn't just formulas. It's the language the universe speaks. We made it visible.

branches.sectionSubtitle: Start with what exists. The rest is on the way.

classical-mechanics.title: CLASSICAL MECHANICS
classical-mechanics.subtitle: The physics of cannonballs, planets, and pendulums.
classical-mechanics.description: Newton's legacy: forces, motion, and the machinery of the everyday world. Pendulums that keep perfect time, planets that sweep out equal areas, cannonballs that trace parabolas. The math is three hundred years old and still runs the world you live in.

electromagnetism.title: ELECTROMAGNETISM
electromagnetism.subtitle: Light, charge, and the field that holds the world together.
electromagnetism.description: Maxwell's four equations — the most important four lines in physics — unify electricity, magnetism, and light into a single field theory. Every radio, screen, wire, and star depends on them.

thermodynamics.title: THERMODYNAMICS
thermodynamics.subtitle: Heat, entropy, and why time moves forward.
thermodynamics.description: Energy flows one way. Entropy only grows. Out of these two facts come steam engines, refrigerators, the arrow of time, and the heat death of the universe. Thermodynamics is the part of physics that feels most like philosophy.

relativity.title: RELATIVITY
relativity.subtitle: The weirdness that hides at the edges of speed.
relativity.description: Space bends. Time stretches. Mass and energy are the same thing. Einstein's special and general relativity rewrote geometry itself — and predicted everything from GPS corrections to black holes.

quantum.title: QUANTUM MECHANICS
quantum.subtitle: The rules the universe actually runs on.
quantum.description: At small enough scales, the world stops behaving like a world. Particles are waves, measurements disturb reality, and probabilities replace certainties. Every transistor, laser, and MRI machine runs on quantum mechanics.

modern-physics.title: MODERN PHYSICS
modern-physics.subtitle: From the nucleus to the Standard Model and beyond.
modern-physics.description: Where quantum mechanics meets relativity, and where physics meets its open frontier. Particle physics, nuclear physics, cosmology, and the questions we still can't answer about what the universe is made of.

the-simple-pendulum.title: THE SIMPLE PENDULUM
the-simple-pendulum.subtitle: Why every clock that ever ticked ticked the same way.

beyond-small-angles.title: BEYOND SMALL ANGLES
beyond-small-angles.subtitle: What the pendulum hides when you stop making approximations.

oscillators-everywhere.title: OSCILLATORS EVERYWHERE
oscillators-everywhere.subtitle: The equation that runs half of physics.

kepler.title: THE LAWS OF PLANETS
kepler.subtitle: How a German astronomer broke the sky open with three sentences.

universal-gravitation.title: UNIVERSAL GRAVITATION
universal-gravitation.subtitle: The force that holds the Moon is the force that drops the apple.

energy-in-orbit.title: ENERGY IN ORBIT
energy-in-orbit.subtitle: One equation tells you the speed at every point in space.

tides-and-three-body.title: TIDES AND THE THREE-BODY PROBLEM
tides-and-three-body.subtitle: When gravity gets complicated, the universe gets interesting.

motion-in-a-straight-line.title: MOTION IN A STRAIGHT LINE
motion-in-a-straight-line.subtitle: Position, velocity, acceleration — the three questions that gave physics its language.

vectors-and-projectile-motion.title: VECTORS AND PROJECTILE MOTION
vectors-and-projectile-motion.subtitle: How Galileo split one hard problem into two easy ones — and why a cannonball traces a parabola.

newtons-three-laws.title: NEWTON'S THREE LAWS
newtons-three-laws.subtitle: The three sentences that still run the world.

friction-and-drag.title: FRICTION AND DRAG
friction-and-drag.subtitle: The forces that quietly take the energy away — and, in doing so, run half of everyday physics.

energy-and-work.title: ENERGY AND WORK
energy-and-work.subtitle: Work, kinetic energy, and potential energy.

momentum-and-collisions.title: MOMENTUM AND COLLISIONS
momentum-and-collisions.subtitle: Elastic, inelastic, and the center of mass.

noethers-theorem.title: NOETHER'S THEOREM
noethers-theorem.subtitle: Symmetry and conservation are the same thing.

torque-and-rotational-dynamics.title: TORQUE AND ROTATIONAL DYNAMICS
torque-and-rotational-dynamics.subtitle: Forces that turn instead of push.

moment-of-inertia.title: MOMENT OF INERTIA
moment-of-inertia.subtitle: Why a hollow sphere rolls slower than a solid one.

the-wobbling-earth.title: THE WOBBLING EARTH
the-wobbling-earth.subtitle: Precession, nutation, and the long memory of the planet.

the-wave-equation.title: THE WAVE EQUATION
the-wave-equation.subtitle: The equation that runs every wave there is.

standing-waves-and-modes.title: STANDING WAVES AND MODES
standing-waves-and-modes.subtitle: Why guitar strings only sing certain notes.

doppler-and-shock-waves.title: DOPPLER AND SHOCK WAVES
doppler-and-shock-waves.subtitle: What changes when the source moves.

dispersion-and-group-velocity.title: DISPERSION AND GROUP VELOCITY
dispersion-and-group-velocity.subtitle: When different frequencies travel at different speeds.

viscosity-and-reynolds-number.title: VISCOSITY AND REYNOLDS NUMBER
viscosity-and-reynolds-number.subtitle: Honey, water, and the line between them.

turbulence.title: TURBULENCE
turbulence.subtitle: The last unsolved problem of classical physics.

the-lagrangian.title: THE LAGRANGIAN
the-lagrangian.subtitle: Mechanics rewritten as energy minus energy.

the-hamiltonian.title: THE HAMILTONIAN
the-hamiltonian.subtitle: Position and momentum, two halves of the same thing.

phase-space.title: PHASE SPACE
phase-space.subtitle: The space where every possible motion lives.

physicists.title: The people behind the laws.
physicists.subtitle: Galileo timed a chandelier against his pulse. Kepler ground eight years into the motion of Mars. The site's cast, in brief.

featured.title: Start here: The Pendulum.
featured.body1: The pendulum is the first serious toy physics ever gave us. It was how Galileo discovered that time could be measured by motion, and how three hundred years of clocks kept pace with the turning of the Earth.
featured.body2: Its motion looks simple — a bob swinging back and forth — but hiding inside that swing is the most important equation in all of oscillations. Pull it out, and you get music, lasers, bridges, and the guts of quantum mechanics.
featured.meta: 5 MIN READ · 5 LIVE FIGURES · CLASSICAL MECHANICS

dictionary.title: The shared vocabulary.
dictionary.subtitle: Instruments, concepts, phenomena, and units — the words that keep coming back across every chapter.

rules.label: 01 · ACCURACY
rules.title: Real math, not cartoons.
rules.body: Every animation is driven by a unit-tested physics solver — odex for ODEs, Newton-Raphson for Kepler's equation, analytic solutions where they exist. If a number appears on screen, it's correct to at least three decimals.

rules.label: 02 · INTUITION
rules.title: Feel it before you prove it.
rules.body: We show you the idea first, with moving pictures, and the math second. No formulas without a picture. No picture without the math underneath.

rules.label: 03 · OPEN
rules.title: Free and open source.
rules.body: Every line of code, every equation, every solver — open source. Fork it, embed it, improve it. Society gets smarter when physics gets easier.
rules.cta: Star on GitHub — rate the project
